title: To A Captious Critic
content:
Dear critic, who my lightness so deplores,
    Would I might study to be prince of bores,
    Right wisely would I rule that dull estate--
    But, sir, I may not, till you abdicate.
meaning:
Paul Laurence Dunbar's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To A Captious Critic"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
